message: agent1: implementing TTL support for cache layers (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py). Adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper. Will touch these two files only.

agent2 here: implementing on-disk compression (magic-header pickle) + cull_limit/eviction_policy knobs for dspy.clients.cache.Cache and configure_cache(). Files: dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py. Will only touch these two files.

agent1: done with TTL feature. Changes in dspy/clients/cache.py: Cache.__init__ now takes new ttl param (last positional, appended after memory_max_entries) + stores self.disk_cache_dir/self.disk_size_limit_bytes/self.memory_max_entries/self.ttl; imports TTLCache; memory_cache branches to TTLCache when ttl set; Cache.get() calls self.disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set; Cache.put() uses self.disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=self.ttl) when ttl set else dict assign. In dspy/clients/__init__.py: configure_cache() gained ttl param passed to Cache(...), added new set_ttl(seconds) function, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None, __all__ includes 'set_ttl'. If your cull_limit/eviction_policy/compression work touches Cache.__init__ signature or put()/get(), please rebase on top of these changes (ttl should stay the last param unless we agree on ordering) - happy to coordinate kwarg order so both features compose cleanly.

agent2 ack: I added compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy as new keyword params (with defaults) to Cache.__init__ right after memory_max_entries, plus Cache.set_compression(codec) helper. I only touch put()'s disk-write line (wrap value via _encode_disk_value when self.compress is not None) and get()'s disk-read line (unwrap via _decode_disk_value, backward-compatible with header-less legacy entries). I don't touch memory_cache type/TTLCache logic. My param order: enable_disk_cache, enable_memory_cache, disk_cache_dir, disk_size_limit_bytes, memory_max_entries, compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy. Since all new params are keyword w/ defaults, order shouldn't matter for correctness even if ttl is appended after mine. We appear to be on separate repo copies (my working dir shows no ttl changes), so if a human merges both patches, they'll just need to combine the two sets of new __init__ kwargs and reconcile put()/get() bodies (mine wraps the value, yours branches disk_cache.set(...) vs dict-assign) — that combination should compose fine since they touch different aspects (byte encoding vs TTL expiry).
